channel.title: Health
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Health, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Meghan Trainor on Building Confidence and Healthy Habits
item.description: Meghan shares how sheâs tuning out criticism, prioritizing her health, and building habits that help her feel stronger than ever.

item.title: What Happens to Your Body When You Drink Enough Water
Suicide rates have declined since the launch of 988 suicide hotline, study finds
item.description: Suicide rates among teens and young adults declined in the two years after the launch of the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline.

item.title: 8 Ways to Get the Protein You Need
Eating This Can Increase Dementia Risk By 43%, New Study Shows
Adding This One Thing To Your Diet Can Lower Blood Pressure, Study Finds
item.description: Plus, it's good for your gut health.

item.title: These 3 Nutrients Improve Bone Health & Lower Fracture Risk, Study Shows
item.description: And increasing your intake of them is simple.

item.title: This âMaster Antioxidantâ May Be The Key To Cellular Longevity, Study Finds
In A Funk? This Social "Vitamin" Might Be The Best Medicine
item.description: A trip to the movies? Yes, please

item.title: Trump administration eases rules on some marijuana categories. Here's what to know
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said he is immediately moving medical marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III, which includes drugs like ketami...

item.title: How mosquitoes â and malaria â helped shape the whereabouts of early humankind
item.description: A new study looks at an unexpected force that played a critical role in shaping the lives of ancient humans.

item.title: Two startlingly different views on long-awaited data on America's anti-HIV efforts
item.description: After a year without data, the State Department released figures on PEPFAR, the program launched by George W. Bush and credited with saving millions o...

item.title: The FDA gives the green light to the first gene therapy for deafness
item.description: The treatment, developed by Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, is for a very rare form of deafness. But it represents a medical milestone.
